title: Gymnadenia chlorantha
taxonomy: Gymnadenia chlorantha is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the genus Gymnadenia. Within this classification, Gymnadenia chlorantha is often referred to as the summer-respiring orchid. This species is native to Europe and is commonly found in woodland and mountainous regions. Gymnadenia chlorantha is further divided into several subspecies, each with unique characteristics.

intro: Gymnadenia chlorantha, also known as the green-fringed orchid, is a perennial orchid species native to Europe and Western Asia. The green-fringed orchid is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to aid in respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's bioactive constituents include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with green-fringed orchid include infusions, tinctures, and dried extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues and other ailments.
